document.title: StickerDB Integration Guide for Execution Satellite
document.purpose: Tell the StickerDB implementation agent exactly how to submit jobs to the current Execution Satellite application and consume its responses.
author: Lion Kimbro
Codex

implementation-status.execution-model: Human-gated. The existence of a request does not cause immediate execution. The operator prepares Leonardo Design Studio and explicitly starts the pending queue.

recommended-reading-for-stickerdb-coding.reason: Current implementation-facing instructions for integrating StickerDB with the running satellite.
Requester-owned external job-cell structure, ownership, and lifecycle.
Request and response JSON meanings and structures.
Architectural purpose, human-gating, ownership, failure, expiration, and response-honesty principles.

stickerdb-configuration.principle: StickerDB should be configured with one deployment anchor: the lionscliapp project directory belonging to Execution Satellite.
rules: StickerDB should not hard-code the execution-satellite repository location.
StickerDB should not separately configure the inbox when it is the conventional inbox child of the configured project directory.
The configured value should be an absolute path after lionscliapp resolves execpath.*.
StickerDB should validate that the configured project directory exists and that its inbox child is available or can be created according to the agreed deployment policy.
StickerDB normally communicates by writing files and does not need to launch the satellite process.
If a future feature launches or focuses Execution Satellite, the working directory is the parent of the configured .execution-satellite directory.
stickerdb-configuration.naming-note: The actual lionscliapp project-directory name is .execution-satellite, with a hyphen.

important-boundary.statement: StickerDB owns its job cells. Execution Satellite owns only its inbox copies and local run records.
stickerdb-owns: jobctrl/response.json after the satellite writes it.
Archiving or deleting the requester-side job cell.
satellite-may: Read full absolute input paths named by the request.
Write full absolute output paths named by the request.
Keep and clean its own inbox copy, InputLog reports, logs, and terminal records.
satellite-must-not-be-expected-to: Delete StickerDB job cells or requester-owned files.
Execute immediately when a request appears.
Prove visual quality or physical print correctness.
Return a response for a malformed request that cannot be normalized.
Return a response merely because an already-expired request was discovered.

submission-procedure.summary: Create the complete requester-owned job cell first, then atomically place a copy of request.json into the satellite inbox.

steps.name: allocate-unique-job-id
steps.instruction: Create a new stable job_id for this submission. Do not reuse a job_id from an earlier submission, including retries or re-ups.

steps.name: create-job-cell
steps.instruction: Create jobctrl, request-args, and response-data under a StickerDB-owned job cell.

steps.name: prepare-inputs-and-destinations
steps.instruction: Write or copy all requester-owned input files. Decide the absolute output paths and absolute response_path.

steps.name: write-requester-request
steps.instruction: Write the complete request to the job cell's jobctrl/request.json.

steps.name: submit-atomically
steps.instruction: Copy the complete request JSON into the satellite inbox using a temporary filename that does not end in .json, then atomically rename it to a unique final filename ending in .json.

steps.name: wait-asynchronously
steps.instruction: Return control to StickerDB. Poll or later check response_path; do not block the StickerDB UI while waiting for human-gated execution.

steps.name: consume-response
steps.instruction: When response_path exists and parses as JSON, verify schema, job_id, and job before applying the result.

steps.name: own-cleanup
steps.instruction: StickerDB decides when to archive, re-up, or delete the requester-side job cell.

submission-procedure.atomic-submission-reason: The satellite scans every *.json file in the inbox derived from the configured Execution Satellite project directory. A temporary extension prevents it from reading a partially copied request.
submission-procedure.filename-note: The satellite does not derive identity from the inbox filename; job_id inside the request is authoritative. A unique job-based filename is recommended for legibility and collision avoidance.

rules: The request must be a JSON object.
job_id must be a non-empty string and unique for this submission.
created_at and expires_at must be ISO-8601 timestamps with timezones.
expires_at must be later than created_at.
Every input path, output path, and response_path must be absolute.
The request should be treated as immutable after submission.

required-input.sticker_image_path: Absolute path to the requester-owned sticker image.

required-output.lds_file_path: Absolute path where the satellite is expected to leave the generated LDS file.

layout_sticker_to_lds.success-observation: The InputLog recording completed normally and lds_file_path exists.

required-input.lds_file_path: Absolute path to the requester-owned LDS file to print.

print_lds_file.success-observation: The InputLog recording completed normally. This does not independently prove physical print quality.

required-consumer-checks: The file parses as a JSON object.
ok and status are interpreted together.

done.meaning: InputLog reached a normal completion report and any requested output file was observed to exist.

failed.meaning: The job failed, the operator chose to fail it, the InputLog report was invalid or abnormal, a requested output was missing, or an earlier job caused the operator to fail the entire queue.

interrupted.meaning: InputLog reported physical user interruption and the operator chose not to retry the job.

response-contract.honesty-rule: Treat observations as facts the satellite could directly observe. A done print response means InputLog completed normally, not that the physical print was inspected and approved.

queue-and-failure-behavior.normal: The satellite writes the response and proceeds to the next pending job.
queue-and-failure-behavior.abnormal: The satellite stops the queue and asks the operator to retry the current job, fail the current job and continue, or fail the entire queue.
queue-and-failure-behavior.retry-current-job: The same submitted request is attempted again locally. StickerDB does not submit another file for this operator retry.
queue-and-failure-behavior.fail-current-job: The satellite writes a failed or interrupted response for the current job and continues.
queue-and-failure-behavior.fail-entire-queue: The satellite writes the current failure response and writes failed responses for the remaining jobs in that launched queue.
queue-and-failure-behavior.important-idempotency-note: Once the satellite has a terminal local record for a job_id, an inbox request with that job_id is no longer pending. To re-up or resubmit work from StickerDB, create a new job_id and a new request.

expiration-and-timeouts.satellite-behavior: A request already past expires_at is displayed as expired and is not run.
expiration-and-timeouts.response-behavior: The current implementation does not write an expired response merely upon discovering an already-expired inbox request.
stickerdb-guidance: Treat absence of response before expires_at as pending human-gated work.
After expires_at, StickerDB may mark the request stale and offer to re-up it.
A re-up must create a new job_id, new timestamps, and a new inbox submission.
Do not use a short synchronous timeout to classify the satellite as failed; execution depends on human readiness.

stickerdb-implementation-checklist: Create a small satellite integration module isolated from StickerDB UI and database logic.
Declare execpath.execution-satellite-project-dir in StickerDB and let the user persist it with sticker-db set.
Derive the submission inbox as the inbox child of the configured Execution Satellite project directory.
Create requester-owned job cells and absolute paths.
Write requester request.json before submitting its copy.
Submit through temporary-file plus atomic rename.
Use a unique job_id for every submission and re-up.
Persist enough StickerDB state to reconnect a business operation to job_id and response_path after restart.
Poll response_path through the Tkinter event loop or another non-blocking periodic mechanism.
Validate response schema, job_id, and job at the castle gate.
Represent pending, done, failed, interrupted, stale, and malformed-response states honestly in the UI.
Leave requester-side cleanup under StickerDB control.
non-contractual-internals: Everything in the configured Execution Satellite project directory other than the inbox submission boundary is private to the satellite unless a later specification explicitly says otherwise.
StickerDB should not read, enumerate, or depend on the satellite-owned runs directory.
StickerDB should not invoke InputLog directly.
StickerDB should not depend on the satellite's hashed local run-directory names.
StickerDB should not infer completion from disappearance or persistence of the inbox copy.
The response_path callback is the requester-visible completion channel.
